observations: Delta is +0.00 because this iteration compares the updated skill against the iteration-1 skill (both know the V2 format), unlike iteration-1's +0.92 delta against no skill. Zero regression across the three package evals is the intended outcome of this comparison.

eval-3 (ramen-shop-single-shot) did not discriminate: the old skill's agent also delivered a bare prompt with no actions.json, correctly overriding the old SKILL.md's always-emit-package instruction based on the user's explicit 'no action keys' phrasing. The new workflow step 0 makes this a rule rather than a judgment call, which should matter for less explicit phrasings ('just a quick prompt for X') that this eval doesn't cover.

Both ramen prompts are nearly identical in shape and quality (109 vs 111 words, same regime, same freeze-clause discipline), suggesting the single-shot craft was already fully carried by the base-prompt sections of the skill.

Token cost is flat: with_skill runs averaged ~45k tokens vs ~43k for old_skill across the eight runs; the single-shot runs were ~20% cheaper than package runs for both configurations (no actions.json to author).

All 27 assertions passed in both configurations, so none of the current assertions discriminate between skill versions; the suite now guards regressions rather than measuring lift. A harsher single-shot phrasing eval (implicit, not explicit, single-prompt intent) would be the next discriminating test.
